title: Migrate all sensitive connection strings and secrets to Kubernetes Secrets and enable encryption at rest
description: Refactor all Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Helm values that currently store sensitive connection strings (including database URLs, Redis URLs, and API keys) in plaintext or base64-encoded placeholders. Move all such data to Kubernetes Secrets. Enable etcd encryption at rest for all secret data. Integrate External Secrets Operator (ESO) to source secrets from a managed secrets store (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, or Cloudflare Workers KV). Update all deployments to reference secrets via envFrom or projected volumes. Add .gitignore rules to prevent accidental commit of values-secrets.yaml. Upgrade the secret rotation CronJob to perform actual automated rotation using ESO's rotation features.
subtasks: Identify all ConfigMaps and Helm values containing sensitive data (database URLs, Redis URLs, API keys, JWT secrets)
Migrate all such data to Kubernetes Secrets and remove from ConfigMaps/values files
Enable etcd encryption at rest using Kubernetes EncryptionConfiguration
Deploy and configure External Secrets Operator (ESO) with a supported secrets manager
Update all service deployments to reference secrets via envFrom or projected volumes
Add .gitignore rules for values-secrets.yaml and any other local secret files
Upgrade the secret rotation CronJob to use ESO's rotation and validate rotation works
Test: Attempt to access secrets via ConfigMap and confirm access is denied; verify secrets are encrypted at rest
Run a security scan to confirm no secrets are present in ConfigMaps or unencrypted locations
acceptance_criteria: No sensitive connection strings or secrets are present in any ConfigMap or Helm values file
All secrets are stored in Kubernetes Secrets and are encrypted at rest
External Secrets Operator is deployed and functional, sourcing secrets from a managed store
All deployments reference secrets via envFrom or projected volumes
values-secrets.yaml and similar files are gitignored and not present in the repo
Secret rotation CronJob performs actual rotation and is validated in logs
Security scan (e.g., kubesec, kube-hunter) confirms no plaintext secrets in ConfigMaps
Manual inspection of etcd data shows secrets are encrypted

title: Enforce mTLS and strong authentication for all internal GDPR endpoints
description: Strengthen the authentication and authorization of all /internal/gdpr/export/:id and /internal/gdpr/delete/:id endpoints across all services. Replace the current network-level enforcement and weak header checks with mutual TLS (mTLS) between services, enforced via Cilium or a service mesh. Replace the X-Internal-Request header with a shared HMAC-signed token or mutual certificate validation. Require a time-limited signed JWT (issued by Morgan's admin skill) as a second factor for GDPR delete operations. Document and test the new defense-in-depth controls.
subtasks: Configure mTLS between all services for /internal/* endpoints using Cilium or a service mesh
Replace X-Internal-Request header checks with HMAC-signed tokens or mutual certificate validation
Implement time-limited signed JWT requirement for GDPR delete endpoints, issued by Morgan's admin skill
Update all service code to validate the new authentication mechanism
Update CiliumNetworkPolicy to only allow mTLS-authenticated traffic to /internal/* endpoints
Test: Attempt GDPR delete from an unauthorized pod and confirm access is denied
Run OWASP ZAP scan against /internal/gdpr endpoints to verify authentication cannot be bypassed
acceptance_criteria: All /internal/gdpr/export and /internal/gdpr/delete endpoints require mTLS and a valid HMAC or JWT
GDPR delete requires a time-limited signed JWT from Morgan's admin skill
No service accepts requests to /internal/gdpr endpoints without proper authentication
CiliumNetworkPolicy enforces mTLS for all /internal/* traffic
Automated and manual tests confirm unauthorized access is blocked
OWASP ZAP scan shows no authentication bypass vulnerabilities

title: Migrate JWT authentication to asymmetric signing and implement per-service audience/issuer validation
description: Replace the shared symmetric JWT_SECRET used across all services with an asymmetric signing algorithm (RS256/ES256). Deploy a centralized identity provider to issue JWTs. Update all services to validate JWTs using the public key, and enforce issuer (iss) and audience (aud) claim validation. Implement a JWKS endpoint for key rotation. As a short-term mitigation, add aud claim checks to prevent tokens issued for one service from being used against another.
subtasks: Deploy a centralized identity provider (e.g., Auth0, Keycloak, or custom) to issue JWTs with RS256/ES256
Update all services to validate JWTs using the public key and verify iss and aud claims
Implement a JWKS endpoint for key rotation and configure all services to use it
Add aud claim checks to all services as an immediate mitigation
Update service documentation and environment variable configuration for new JWT validation
Test: Attempt to use a JWT issued for one service against another and confirm it is rejected
Run integration tests for JWT validation, including key rotation scenarios
acceptance_criteria: All services validate JWTs using asymmetric keys and verify issuer and audience claims
A JWKS endpoint is available and used for key rotation
Tokens issued for one service cannot be used against another
Short-term mitigation (aud claim check) is in place until full migration is complete
Integration tests for JWT validation and key rotation pass
Manual attempts to forge or reuse JWTs across services are rejected

title: Pin all container images to digests and implement CI container image scanning
description: Update all Kubernetes Deployments and Dockerfiles to pin container images (including Signal-CLI and all service images) to specific digests (e.g., sha256:...). Add container image scanning (e.g., Trivy, Grype) to the CI pipeline for all images, including third-party and base images. Consider building a custom Signal-CLI image from source with a verified base image. Apply the same image pinning policy to all service Dockerfiles.
subtasks: Update all Deployment manifests to use image digests instead of :latest tags
Update all service Dockerfiles to use pinned base image digests
Add container image scanning (Trivy, Grype) to the CI pipeline for all images
Build and publish a custom Signal-CLI image from source with a verified base image
Document the image pinning and scanning policy in the security runbook
Test: Run Trivy/Grype scans on all images and verify no critical vulnerabilities
Attempt to deploy with an unpinned image and confirm CI blocks the deployment
acceptance_criteria: All container images are pinned to specific digests in manifests and Dockerfiles
CI pipeline runs image scanning on every build and blocks on critical findings
Signal-CLI and all service images are built from verified sources
Documentation exists for image pinning and scanning policy
Manual attempts to deploy :latest or unpinned images are blocked by CI

title: Enforce TLS for all database connections between application pods and PostgreSQL
description: Configure the CloudNativePG Cluster CR to require TLS (hostssl) for all connections. Update all application connection strings to include sslmode=verify-full and mount the CNPG-generated CA certificate in each service pod. Add tests to verify that non-TLS connections are rejected.
subtasks: Update CNPG Cluster CR to require TLS (hostssl) for all connections
Generate and distribute the CA certificate to all service pods via Kubernetes Secrets or ConfigMaps
Update all service connection strings to use sslmode=verify-full
Mount the CA certificate in all service pods and update application configuration to use it
Test: Attempt to connect to PostgreSQL without TLS and confirm connection is rejected
Run integration tests to verify all services can connect with TLS
acceptance_criteria: All database connections between application pods and PostgreSQL use TLS
Connection strings use sslmode=verify-full and reference the correct CA certificate
Non-TLS connection attempts are rejected by PostgreSQL
Integration tests for TLS connectivity pass for all services

title: Implement project-wide input validation and enforce parameterized SQL queries
description: Establish a project-wide policy requiring all SQL queries to use parameterized queries (sqlx bind params in Rust, pgx named params in Go). Add compile-time query checking (sqlx::query! macro) where possible. Add integration tests that attempt SQL injection payloads on search endpoints. Consider adding a WAF rule at the Cloudflare level for common injection patterns.
subtasks: Review all dynamic SQL query construction and refactor to use parameterized queries
Add compile-time query checking (e.g., sqlx::query! macro) in Rust services
Add integration tests with SQL injection payloads for all search/filter endpoints
Document input validation and SQL parameterization policy for all developers
Coordinate with infrastructure to add a WAF rule at Cloudflare for common SQL injection patterns
Run OWASP ZAP scan against all API endpoints to verify no SQL injection vulnerabilities
acceptance_criteria: All SQL queries use parameterized/bound parameters; no string concatenation for user input
Compile-time query checking is enabled where supported
Integration tests with SQL injection payloads return safe responses (no injection possible)
WAF rules are in place at Cloudflare for common SQLi patterns
OWASP ZAP scan shows no SQL injection vulnerabilities

title: Implement field-level encryption for PII and ensure encryption at rest for all databases
description: Enable PostgreSQL Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) if supported by CloudNativePG, or ensure the underlying storage class uses encrypted volumes. For highly sensitive fields (e.g., credit_score, risk_flags, worker payment amounts), implement application-level encryption using pgcrypto's pgp_sym_encrypt/decrypt with a key managed outside the database. Add encryption-at-rest validation to the infrastructure test suite.
subtasks: Enable PostgreSQL TDE or verify storage class uses encrypted volumes for all databases
Identify all PII fields and implement application-level encryption for highly sensitive data using pgcrypto
Store encryption keys outside the database (e.g., in a secrets manager)
Update application code to encrypt/decrypt fields as needed
Add infrastructure tests to validate encryption at rest is enabled
Test: Attempt to read raw disk blocks and confirm data is encrypted
Run a compliance scan to verify encryption at rest for all PII
acceptance_criteria: All databases use TDE or encrypted storage volumes
Highly sensitive PII fields are encrypted at the application level
Encryption keys are managed outside the database and are not accessible to the DB process
Infrastructure tests confirm encryption at rest is enabled
Compliance scan passes for encryption at rest

title: Enable authentication for Valkey in production and restrict access via network policy
description: Enable Valkey AUTH with a strong password stored in a Kubernetes Secret for production. Update all service Redis connection strings to include the password. Ensure the CiliumNetworkPolicy for Valkey allows connections only from the five application services.
subtasks: Configure Valkey to require AUTH with a strong password in production
Store the Valkey password in a Kubernetes Secret and update all service deployments to use it
Update all Redis connection strings to include the password
Update CiliumNetworkPolicy to only allow connections to Valkey from authorized services
Test: Attempt to connect to Valkey without AUTH and confirm access is denied
Run a network scan to verify only authorized pods can connect to Valkey
acceptance_criteria: Valkey requires AUTH in production and only accepts connections with the correct password
All services use the updated connection string with password
CiliumNetworkPolicy restricts Valkey access to authorized services
Unauthorized connection attempts are denied

title: Restrict Morgan's egress network policy to specific ports and paths with rate limiting
description: Update the Morgan CiliumNetworkPolicy to restrict egress to only the specific ports and HTTP paths required by Morgan. Use Cilium L7 policy to limit Morgan to only the necessary API endpoints (e.g., /api/v1/* and /internal/gdpr/*). Add rate limiting at the Cilium layer for Morgan's outbound connections to prevent DoS attacks.
subtasks: Update Morgan's CiliumNetworkPolicy to restrict egress to specific ports and HTTP paths
Implement Cilium L7 policy to allow only /api/v1/* and /internal/gdpr/* endpoints
Add rate limiting rules at the Cilium layer for Morgan's outbound connections
Test: Attempt to access unauthorized endpoints from Morgan and confirm access is denied
Simulate high-rate outbound traffic from Morgan and verify rate limiting is enforced
acceptance_criteria: Morgan's egress is restricted to only required ports and HTTP paths
Cilium L7 policy is in place and blocks unauthorized requests
Rate limiting is enforced at the network layer
Unauthorized or excessive outbound traffic from Morgan is blocked

title: Implement structured security event logging and audit trail across all services
description: Add structured security event logging to all services. Log all authentication failures, admin-level operations, GDPR operations, and Morgan tool invocations with user/session context. Ship these logs to a dedicated security index in Loki with extended retention. Add alerting rules for high auth failure rates, GDPR deletes, and admin token usage from unexpected sources.
subtasks: Implement structured security event logging in all services for authentication failures, admin operations, GDPR actions, and Morgan tool invocations
Configure log shipping to a dedicated security index in Loki with longer retention
Add alerting rules in Loki for >10 auth failures per minute, any GDPR delete, and admin token usage from unexpected sources
Test: Trigger authentication failures and verify logs and alerts are generated
Test: Perform GDPR delete and verify audit log and alert are generated
acceptance_criteria: All relevant security events are logged in a structured format
Logs are shipped to a dedicated security index in Loki with extended retention
Alerting rules are in place and trigger on defined security events
Manual and automated tests confirm logs and alerts are generated as expected

title: Mandate dependency pinning, lockfiles, and supply chain scanning for all services
description: Require committed lockfiles for all Node.js and Go services (package-lock.json, bun.lockb, go.sum). Add govulncheck to the Go CI pipeline. Add SBOM generation (syft or cyclonedx) as a CI step for all services. Pin all dependency versions to exact versions in package.json. Add Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency update PRs.
subtasks: Ensure all Node.js and Go services have committed lockfiles (package-lock.json, bun.lockb, go.sum)
Add govulncheck to the Go CI pipeline
Add SBOM generation (syft or cyclonedx) to the CI pipeline for all services
Pin all dependency versions to exact versions in package.json and go.mod
Configure Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency update PRs
Test: Remove a lockfile and verify CI fails
Run supply chain scan and verify no critical vulnerabilities
acceptance_criteria: All services have committed lockfiles and pinned dependency versions
CI pipeline runs govulncheck and SBOM generation for all services
Dependabot or Renovate is configured and active
Supply chain scans pass with no critical findings

title: Mandate Stripe webhook signature validation using official library and add tampering tests
description: Require all Stripe webhook endpoints to use the stripe-rust crate's built-in webhook::Webhook::construct_event() function for signature validation. Add integration tests that send requests with tampered bodies and valid-looking signature headers to verify rejection. Document the requirement to read the raw body bytes before any JSON parsing middleware.
subtasks: Update Stripe webhook handler to use stripe-rust's construct_event() for signature validation
Ensure raw request body is read before any deserialization or middleware processing
Add integration tests that send tampered bodies with valid-looking signatures and verify rejection
Document the correct implementation pattern for future developers
Run OWASP ZAP scan against the webhook endpoint
acceptance_criteria: All Stripe webhook endpoints use official library for signature validation
Raw body is read before any parsing or middleware
Integration tests with tampered payloads are rejected with 400
Documentation is updated with correct implementation pattern
OWASP ZAP scan shows no signature validation bypass

title: Implement tool-level authorization and human-in-the-loop controls for Morgan AI agent
description: Implement tool-level authorization controls for the Morgan AI agent. Use scoped JWTs with specific permissions per tool. Require human-in-the-loop confirmation via Signal for high-risk operations (GDPR delete, large invoice creation, social media publishing). Add rate limits on Morgan's tool invocations and log all invocations with full request/response payloads to the audit schema.
subtasks: Implement scoped JWTs for Morgan with specific permissions per tool
Require human confirmation via Signal for high-risk operations before execution
Add rate limiting for Morgan's tool invocations (e.g., max 5 GDPR deletes per hour)
Log all tool invocations with full request/response payloads to the audit schema
Test: Attempt to invoke a tool without the required scope and confirm access is denied
Simulate high-frequency tool invocations and verify rate limiting is enforced
Perform a high-risk operation and verify human confirmation is required and logged
acceptance_criteria: Morgan uses scoped JWTs for tool-level authorization
High-risk operations require human-in-the-loop confirmation
Rate limits are enforced on tool invocations
All tool invocations are logged to the audit schema
Unauthorized or excessive tool invocations are blocked
